message:
v0.3.4: MAD-based burst detection, burst-aware guard, median-rate cross-check

Four production fixes for bursty workload handling:

1. MAD robust rate estimator (ewma.rs): Add median absolute deviation to
   BurstState with robust_upper_bound (median + 3×MAD). is_burst_outlier()
   classifies observations where actual rate exceeds statistical expectation.

2. Burst-aware guard calibration (guardrails.rs, loop_main.rs): Observations
   flagged as burst outliers are treated as "good" — they don't poison the
   calibration window. Prevents guard from permanently entering Fail during
   sustained compilation bursts (was causing 600+ consecutive breaches).

3. Median-rate cross-check (predictive.rs): When EWMA diverges >3× from
   median rate and disk has >25% free, re-project using median rate. If
   median says no danger, return Clear. Eliminates false "disk full in 37m"
   predictions during compilation bursts on machines with 30-50% free.

4. Rate-limited suppression logs (policy.rs): "suppressing fallback" messages
   now rate-limited to once per 300s instead of every scan cycle (was 5-8
   times per 30s cycle on 4 machines).

Also fixes pre-existing test bug: proof_harness failing_guard() had
observation_count=25 but check_guard_triggers() requires >=30.

1847 tests pass (3 pre-existing macOS platform failures).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.3"
+version = "0.3.4"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
